title: Causal Network Map: interactive viewer and replication package for "Qualitative Shock Mapping for Quantitative Production Network Models: Evidence from Iran-Related Hostilities"
description:
Interactive lead-lag network viewer and a curated replication package for a news-based sectoral shock-mapping framework applied to Iran-related hostilities (Kim & Erokhin, IIASA).

Contents. Derived daily sectoral indicators and within-day share-transformed series by language; article-level sector-probability vectors from a lexical (TF-IDF) and a zero-shot large-language-model classifier; full pairwise Toda-Yamamoto lead-lag edge tables with false-discovery-rate flags; the world-average input-output coefficient matrix and input-output alignment statistics; the 50-sector ICIO V1 lookup; the LLM classification prompt; and all analysis and figure-generation scripts.

Raw article text is not redistributed for copyright reasons; only derived quantities and non-text metadata are included, so the pipeline can be audited and re-run on independently collected text.

Licensing. Code (the viewer and the Python scripts under replication/code/) is released under the MIT License. Datasets under replication/data/ are released under the Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License (CC BY 4.0).
